markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games has delayed Grand Theft Auto VI multiple times, with the latest official release set for November 19, 2026, after earlier targets of fall 2025 and May 2026 slipped due to polish needs. This extended window broadens opportunities for unrelated cultural and tech milestones to resolve first, shaping trader focus on high-profile releases like new albums or AI models such as GPT-6. Ongoing GTA Online updates and trailer speculation add industry momentum, while the November date locks in a firm resolution horizon for markets tracking pre-launch events amid fluctuating entertainment cycles.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials in Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrial on the remaining low-level rape charge have driven trader consensus toward no additional prison time, now implied at nearly 80%. The May 15, 2026, deadlocked jury marked the third inconclusive outcome on that allegation after the 2020 conviction was overturned, leaving prosecutors with a June decision on pursuing a fourth trial. At 74 and already credited with more than six years served—exceeding the four-year maximum for the charge—Weinstein faces documented health challenges while appealing his separate 16-year California sentence. These factors, combined with time-served credits and the limited upside for further incarceration, underpin the market’s pronounced tilt despite ongoing appeals and unresolved proceedings.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Carolina Hurricanes enter the 2026 Stanley Cup Final as slight favorites in trader consensus, reflecting their league-best 113 regular-season points, dominant Eastern Conference playoff run that includes a 4-1 series win over Montreal and an overall 12-1 postseason mark heading into the championship round. Elite goaltending from Frederik Andersen, depth scoring, and strong puck-possession metrics have sustained momentum through the playoffs. The Vegas Golden Knights counter with Western Conference supremacy, including a sweep of Colorado in the conference finals for their third Final appearance in nine franchise seasons, plus proven playoff experience and contributions from key forwards. Both clubs feature balanced rosters suited for a best-of-seven series, keeping the implied probabilities closely contested around 58-42.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The San Antonio Spurs hold a clear edge in trader consensus for the 2026 NBA title, fueled by Victor Wembanyama’s two-way dominance, a deep young core featuring Stephon Castle, and their Game 7 victory over the defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder to reach the Finals. New York’s 35.8 percent implied probability reflects the Knicks’ record postseason run—an 11-game winning streak, elite net rating, and Eastern Conference dominance behind Jalen Brunson and Karl-Anthony Towns—but the road schedule and Spurs’ home advantage in the series tilt market pricing toward San Antonio. Recent roster continuity and playoff momentum for both clubs define the closely contested matchup.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping’s consolidated authority within the Chinese Communist Party and central institutions underpins the 93.3% trader consensus against his removal before 2027. Recent military leadership changes in early 2026 have further centralized control over the Central Military Commission, while no designated successor has emerged and elite factions show no visible opposition ahead of the 21st Party Congress. Institutional norms and the absence of mechanisms for early transition reinforce expectations that Xi will pursue a fourth term in late 2027, with few credible catalysts for change in the interim.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nominee at 25.1 percent, reflecting his California governorship, extensive fundraising reach, and sustained national visibility through criticism of the Trump administration.
